title: Website + automations for local businesses — pre-Rankett ground game
html:
TL;DR

  • The bridge document between "I should sell websites to local businesses" and "I should build rankett as a white-label partner" — Oct 2025, the wedge thesis is forming but still pointed at end-clients, not agencies.
  • The lean stack: Google Maps free scraper → email extractor ($10/mo for 5K) → validation → brevo ($8/mo) for sends. Pay-for-tool (socleads $60/mo) deferred until first paying client.
  • The offer: $45/mo to host + optimize for AI search results, not just Google. Pitch: "It's like having the perfect house for sale with no 'For Sale' sign."
  • Doc contains a long extracted hubspot AEO-strategy LLM prompt — Yahir's prompt-engineering source material for the ai visibility engine he'd build into Rankett.
  • Tactical playbook: gptbot / oai searchbot / perplexitybot enablement, schema-structured "Answer" content, weekly Google/Bing/Apple business-profile syncs.

What this is

Yahir in Oct 2025 working out the business shape that will become rankett. The doc reads as several iteration passes stacked: the FINALE section (his settled cheap stack), the Plan section (offer + outreach + action), the 5-minute call plan (the actual sales script), and a copy-pasted hubspot LLM prompt template he'd use to deeply think about the strategy.

Crucial: this is before the agency-partner pivot. Here, Yahir is still selling directly to local businesses at $45/mo. The category insight is right; the GTM model isn't yet. The pivot to white-labeling for marketing agencies (documented in 2026 04 27 Rankett.com) is what made the unit economics work.

The lean stack

Yahir's specific cheap-to-ship pipeline:

  1. Free Google Maps Easy Scrape extension → domain names
  2. Email enrichment ($10/mo for 5K emails)
  3. Validation — TBD
  4. brevo ($8/mo) for cold-campaign sends
  5. socleads ($60/mo) — deferred until first client lands

The discipline: "need to be a little bit more lean. And scrappy." Pay only when revenue justifies. This is consistent with the no runway discipline thread across other notes.

Three parallel motions: cold outreach, outreach to those without websites, phone calls. "Sell them the destination, not the plane."

The offer

Charge $45/mo to host + optimize for ai search optimization. Specifics:

  • Structured data everywhere
  • Q&A pages answering who/what/where/price/availability
  • Entity linking (brand, owner, neighborhood, services) for AI grounding
  • Clean About/Contact + crawlable details + policies + pricing ranges
  • "For AI" section: canonical answers to top-15 questions, each as a standalone URL using FAQPage / HowTo / Service schema

The 5-minute pitch:

  • Pre-set the prospect's results in chatgpt + gemini + perplexity → show them how invisible they are
  • WHY: "It's like having the perfect house for sale with no 'For Sale' sign. Buyers drive right past without knowing it's available."
  • HOW (technical): gptbot / oai searchbot / perplexitybot enablement, machine-ready schema timestamps, "Answer" content, google business profile + bing places + apple business connect weekly sync
  • WHO: work backwards from converting online search → paying clients

The HubSpot LLM prompt

The latter ~70% of the doc is a copy-paste of hubspot's "AI search optimization strategist" prompt template — Yahir was using it as input scaffolding to think through Rankett's strategy. The template covers content authority, citation optimization, query coverage, source credibility, freshness — the same axes that show up structured-into-tasks in the 2025 12 30 GEO AI AAS Agency SOP doc.

This is a small artifact of how Yahir builds: lift external strategic templates → fill them with his own context → distill into operational SOPs.

Why it matters in the graph

This doc is the precursor in the rankett arc. The technical thesis (open the bots, structure the answers, sync the profiles) is fully formed. What changes between Oct 2025 and the 2026 04 27 Rankett.com partner-program era is the buyer: end-clients (hard to acquire, low LTV) → marketing agencies (network effect, much higher LTV, ghost white label moat).

The Semrush stat Yahir cites — AI search visitors are 4.4x more valuable than traditional organic search visitors — is the unit-economics underpinning of the entire ai search optimization thesis.

Privacy

No credentials in this doc.
